# Frontend Design Rules

## Layout

- Use a clear container strategy and keep hero copy in a readable width.
- Separate information clusters with generous vertical rhythm.
- Keep dense sections organized with obvious alignment anchors.

## Typography

- Use a disciplined type scale with strong heading-to-body contrast.
- Keep body copy readable and avoid long, fatiguing line lengths.
- Limit decorative type moments to places with real emphasis value.

## CTA Strategy

- Use one dominant hero CTA and keep secondary actions visually subordinate.
- Write CTA labels as concrete actions, not vague slogans.

## Components

- Prefer simple, composable surfaces over overloaded cards.
- Use badges, tabs, and proof blocks to support scanning rather than decorate.

## Motion

- Use motion to explain structure or reward interaction, not to fill space.
- Prefer restrained fade, translate, scale, and reveal patterns by default.
- Reserve heavy motion, 3D, or immersive transitions for high-value moments.

## Rendering and Performance

- Treat advanced rendering as optional enhancement, not baseline requirement.
- Keep effects compatible with mobile performance and progressive loading.

## Accessibility

- Maintain contrast, keyboard access, visible focus, and reduced-motion fallbacks.
- Avoid relying on animation or color alone to communicate meaning.
